annotations.label: Determinant as SL_n invariant

anchor.snippet: if we consider the action of the special linear group

annotations.note: Mathlib has `SL_n` and `det_coe : det A = 1`, but does not state the invariance of `det` as a polynomial function under the SL action on matrices.

annotations.label: Representation and induced action on polynomials
annotations.note: Group representations exist as `Representation k G V`, but the induced action on the polynomial ring/symmetric algebra of `V` is not packaged as a single named declaration.

annotations.label: Ring of invariant polynomials

anchor.snippet: With this action it is natural to consider the subspace of all polynomial functions which are invariant

annotations.note: `FixedPoints.subalgebra` gives the fixed subalgebra under any `MulSemiringAction`, which specializes to the ring of invariant polynomials, but no Mathlib decl names this specialization.

annotations.label: First problem of invariant theory
annotations.note: This is an informal/open meta-question (finding generators of the invariant ring) and is not formalized.

annotations.label: Invariants of square matrices under left multiplication

anchor.snippet: the space of square matrices, and the action

annotations.note: No grep hit for a description of the invariant ring of `n×n` matrices under left GL-multiplication in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finite generation for finite groups in characteristic zero

anchor.snippet: answered in the affirmative in a wide generality

annotations.note: Finite generation of the invariant ring `k[V]^G` for finite `G` in characteristic 0 is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finite generation of syzygies via Hilbert's basis theorem

anchor.snippet: is the module of polynomial relations between the basis elements

annotations.note: Hilbert's basis theorem is `Polynomial.isNoetherianRing`, but its application to finite generation of the syzygy module of invariants is not stated.

annotations.label: Main theorem on symmetric functions

anchor.snippet: main theorem on the symmetric functions that described the invariants

annotations.note: The fundamental theorem of symmetric polynomials is formalized via the `esymmAlgHom` algebra isomorphism in `Mathlib.RingTheory.MvPolynomial.Symmetric.FundamentalTheorem`.

annotations.label: Chevalley–Shephard–Todd theorem
annotations.note: No Chevalley–Shephard–Todd or pseudo-reflection development found in Mathlib (only a 1000-theorems entry).

annotations.label: Invariant monomials from a group action

anchor.snippet: Simple examples of invariant theory come from computing the invariant monomials

annotations.note: No Mathlib computation of invariant monomials for the standard small examples (e.g. `ℤ/n` acting diagonally) was found.

annotations.label: Subalgebra of invariants of n-ary r-ics

anchor.snippet: The point is then to define the subalgebra of invariants

annotations.note: The classical invariants of `n`-ary `r`-ic forms under `GL_n` are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Klein's invariant rings of binary polyhedral groups

anchor.snippet: Felix Klein in computing the invariant rings of finite group actions

annotations.note: Klein's invariant rings of binary polyhedral groups (Du Val singularities) are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hilbert's finite generation theorem
annotations.note: Hilbert's theorem on finite generation of invariant rings under `GL_n` (or reductive groups) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reynolds operator properties

anchor.snippet: His proof used the Reynolds operator

annotations.note: For finite groups (with invertible order) `Representation.averageMap` is the Reynolds operator, with projection and module-map properties proved (`averageMap_invariant`, `averageMap_id`, `isProj_averageMap`).

annotations.label: Construction of Reynolds operator

anchor.snippet: Hilbert constructed the Reynolds operator explicitly using Cayley's omega process

annotations.note: Cayley's Ω-process construction of the Reynolds operator for `SL_n` is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Proof of Hilbert's theorem via Reynolds operator

anchor.snippet: Given the Reynolds operator, Hilbert's theorem is proved as follows

annotations.note: The Reynolds-operator-based proof of finite generation of the invariant ring is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hilbert-Nagata theorem
annotations.note: Mathlib has `NoetherNormalization` and mentions Nagata in references, but the Hilbert–Nagata theorem on finite generation of invariants of geometrically reductive groups is not stated.

annotations.label: Finite generation for finite groups of order prime to characteristic
annotations.note: This corollary of Hilbert–Nagata to finite groups with `|G|` invertible is not stated as a Mathlib theorem about the invariant ring.

annotations.label: Finite generation for reductive groups in characteristic zero
annotations.note: Finite generation of the invariant ring under reductive groups in characteristic zero is not formalized in Mathlib.
